Milling Troubleshooter
Tree view (branching map) + OEM actions + best-practice checklist
Tip: Install like an app on iPhone → open in Safari → Share → Add to Home Screen.

Stage details

Downstream stage snapshots (Level 1)

Tap a stage to see its details. Use “Set as Stage” to jump.

Upstream stage snapshots (Level 1)

These stages feed your selected stage.

Cause-chain hint

Quick upstream/downstream propagation hints using your flow connections (Level 1). Enter a trouble keyword to activate.

Downstream tree


      


      

        
Upstream tree

        

      

    


    

      
Recommendations


      
OEM (from your manuals)

      


      
Best-practice checklist

      


      

        Notes: Recommendations are guidance. Always confirm with your OEM manuals and your mill safety procedures.
      

    


    



    
    

      
Mill control

      
Break Release + Distribution Table + Granulation (offline tools)


      

        Break Release (4- & 5-break guideline)
        

          Lab check: % through No.20W (1110µ) after 3 minutes sifting. Use as a starting point — adjust for wheat, extraction (ash), and roll condition.
        


        

          
          
        


        

          
          
        


        

          
          
        


        

          
          
        


        

          
          
        


        


        

          Guideline table (from your photo)

          

            

              
4 break system

              

                
                  

                    Passage
                    Approx. gap (mm)
                    % through No.20W (1110µ) (3 min)
                  

                
                
                  
B10.5030–35

                  
B20.2552–55

                  
B30.1055–58

                  
B4 c.touchingclean bran

                  
B4 f.touchingclean bran

                
              

            


            

              
5 break system

              

                
                  

                    Passage
                    Approx. gap (mm)
                    % through No.20W (1110µ) (3 min)
                  

                
                
                  
B10.6027–30

                  
B20.3050–52

                  
B30.2055–58

                  
B4 c.0.10~40

                  
B4 f.touching— (check bran cleanliness)

                  
B5 c.touchingclean bran

                  
B5 f.touchingclean bran

                
              

            

          


          

            Remarks: these are approximate guidelines. Adjust to conditions like:
            
    
              
  • Wheat varieties (hard/soft) and conditioning
    • 
              
  • Flour extraction rate (ash target)
    • 
              
  • Long or short milling system
    • 
              
  • Condition of roll flutes / wear
    • 
            

          

        


        
Source: your photo “Guidelines for setting the break rolls of a flour mill” (No.20W 1110µ, 3 min).

      


      



        

          Use this section to control the mill day‑to‑day by testing intermediate stocks with a lab/test sifter: break release targets, distribution tables, and a granulation (particle size) curve.
          In your project PDF “طرق تحديد كفاءة طحن القمح”, the “Mill control” chapter includes: testing of intermediate stocks, test sifters, break release adjustment, distribution table, granulation curve, and cumulative ash curve.
        


        

          

            
Targets (project PDF)

            
Typical commercial break release targets


            

              
                

                  Break stage
                  Target break release (%)
                  What “too high / too low” usually means
                

              
              
                
B135–45Too high → bran powdering/ash↑ • Too low → low release/extraction↓

                
B250–60Too high → overloaded sifter/bran cutting • Too low → heavy load downstream

                
B330–40Too high → fine bran • Too low → tail load and poor separation

                
B420–30Too high → late bran damage • Too low → low total extraction

              
            


            

              Tip: when comparing 2 settings, look at both integral break release and cumulative ash. You can have similar total release but different ash (cleaner separation vs bran grinding).
            

          


          

            
Calculator

            
Break release checker


            

              
              
            


            

              
              
            


            

              
              
            


            

              
              
            


            


            

              Integral break release (optional quick sum)
              
Enter B1–B4 break release (%) and this tool sums them (compare to your mill baseline + ash).

              

                
                
                
                
              

              

            


            

              Quick practical checklist (what to change first)
              
    
                
  • Break release low: tighten gap slightly (small steps), verify roll sharpness, check tempering/moisture and feed rate stability.
    • 
                
  • Break release high: open gap slightly, verify differential and feed, check sifter cloth/blinding/brushes; watch ash + bran powdering.
    • 
                
  • Always: keep sampling method consistent (same point, same time, same sieve time). Otherwise the “numbers” will mislead you.
    • 
              

            

          

        


        

          

            
Tool

            
Distribution table builder (from test sifter weights)


            

              Paste your sieve opening (µm) + weight retained (g). Use “Pan” for the bottom tray.
              This produces a distribution table and a granulation curve (cumulative passing).
            


            

              
              

                
                
Format: opening_µm, weight_g (comma / tab / semicolon all accepted).

              

            


            

              
            


            

          


          

            
Chart

            
Granulation curve (cumulative passing)


            

              The curve updates from the distribution table. Use it to compare coarsening/fineness shifts between days, wheat blends, or roll settings.
            


            


            

              Interpretation (rule‑of‑thumb): curve shifts upward = finer stock / higher release; curve shifts downward = coarser stock / lower release.
            

          

        


        

          ملخص عربي (تحكم المطحنة)
          
    
            
  • Break Release (نسبة التنعيم): مؤشر سريع لدرجة تفكيك الاندوسبرم في الكسرات. ارتفاعها جداً غالباً يسبب نخالة مطحونة وارتفاع رماد/نقاط سوداء.
    • 
            
  • جدول التوزيع: يحول أوزان المناخل إلى نسب مئوية وتراكمية للمقارنة بين الأيام والخلطات.
    • 
            
  • منحني التحبحب (Granulation): رسم %المرور التراكمي مقابل فتحة المنخل. يعطيك تشخيص بصري “أنعم أم أخشن”.
    • 
            
  • لازم توحيد طريقة أخذ العينة وزمن النخل حتى تكون النتائج قابلة للمقارنة.
    • 
          

        

      
      

    



      
Reference library (offline)

      
Quick guides + calculators (conditioning, rolls, sieves, cleaning, air systems) added from your project PDFs.

      

        
        

          
          
        

      



      

        1) Wheat conditioning (tempering) – quick guide

        

          Goal: toughen/elasticize bran and mellow endosperm so you get cleaner separation, better release, and fewer specks/ash problems.
        


        

          

            
Bühler – Wheat Conditioning

            
Recommended 1BK moisture & tempering time (typical ranges)


            

              
                

                  Wheat class
                  Moisture at 1BK (%)
                  Tempering time
                

              
              
                
Hard wheat16.0–17.0 (up to 17.5)24–36 h (up to 48 h)

                
Semi‑hard wheat15.5–16.018–24 h

                
Semi‑soft wheat15.0–15.512–18 h

                
Soft wheat14.5–15.06–12 h

              
            


            

              Two‑step tempering (guide): first stage ~2/3 of total time (add water 1–2% below final target), then second stage ~1/3 of total time (add remaining 1–2%).
              Bühler notes 8–12 h is typical for the second step (not longer), and 1BK “short tempering” can be ~0.3–0.5% moisture addition for ~5–10 min.
            

          


          

            
Calculator

            
Water addition calculator


            

              
              
            

            

              
              
            

            

              
              
            


            


            

              Formula (Bühler): Water % = 100 × (F2 − F1) / (100 − F2). Then Water (L/h) ≈ Water fraction × wheat flow (kg/h).
              Tip: always verify dampener calibration and mixing (uneven wetting causes choking + quality swings).
            

          

        



        

          

            
Bühler – Dampening notes

            
High‑speed dampening & water migration

            
    
              
  • Water has strong surface tension (~72.75 mN/m at 20°C). High‑speed dampeners use centrifugal forces to create a thin, uniform water layer on wheat, enabling higher water additions in one pass.
    • 
              
  • Low‑speed dampening (e.g., paddle screw conveyors) typically handles < 3% water addition.
    • 
              
  • High‑speed systems (examples): intensive dampener < 5.5%; tri‑rotor / turbolizer < 7% water addition (design guides).
    • 
              
  • Water migration risk: uneven wetting can cause water migration toward bin outlets → uneven moisture + blocked bin outlets.
    • 
              
  • Humid conditioning bins can encourage bacteria growth; good mass‑flow bin design and aspiration help reduce problem zones.
    • 
            

          


          

            
Troubleshooting tip

            
When to suspect tempering problems

            
    
              
  • Frequent chokes/bridging after dampening or in wet weather.
    • 
              
  • Big swings in break release, ash, or specks after wheat / blend changes.
    • 
              
  • Sticky stock + sieve blinding or sudden load increase in early breaks.
    • 
            

            
Action idea: verify dampener calibration, mixing, and bin discharge (drawdown). For large water additions, consider 2‑step tempering.

          

        


        

          ملخص عربي سريع (تكييف القمح)
          
    
            
  • الهدف من التكييف: جعل النخالة مرنة/قوية وتقليل تفتتها (حتى لا ترتفع نسبة الرماد ولا تظهر نقاط سوداء).
    • 
            
  • القمح القاسي عادة يحتاج رطوبة أعلى ووقت أطول للتكييف مقارنة بالقمح الطري.
    • 
            
  • إذا حصل انسداد/تكتل بعد الترطيب: احتمال توزيع ماء غير متجانس (هجرة الماء) أو زيادة ماء في خطوة واحدة؛ الحل غالباً تحسين الخلط أو استخدام تكييف على مرحلتين.
    • 
            
  • أي تغيير في نوع القمح أو الرطوبة يحتاج إعادة ضبط: الكسرات (Break release) + تنقية السميد (Purifier) + فجوات الرولات.
    • 
          

        

      


      

        2) Roller mill settings (reference)

        

          

            
Arabic project note

            
Break roll gap (example reference values)

            

              
                

                  Break
                  Gap (mm)
                  Gap (inch)
                

              
              
                
B10.500.020

                
B20.150.006

                
B30.090.0035

                
B40.080.003

              
            

            
These are reference starting points (your wheat + your roll condition will move them).

          


          

            
Arabic project note

            
Typical roll speed differential (guide)

            

              
                
SystemTypical differentialWhy

              
              
                
Break (fluted)1 : 2.5More scraping action (clean bran, release endosperm)

                
Reduction / smooth1 : 1.25 to 1 : 1.5Reduce bran cutting while grinding middlings

              
            


            

              Roll disposition (flutes): many flour mills use “back‑to‑back” style to reduce bran cutting; semolina mills may use different dispositions depending on target.
            

          

        


        

          

            
Arabic project note

            
Typical flute count for break rolls (guide)

            

              
                
BreakFlutes (teeth) per inchApprox. per cm

              
              
                
B1124.7

                
B2145.5

                
B3187.1

                
B426–3010.2–11.8

              
            

            
Use as a reference. Actual choice depends on wheat hardness, desired granulation, and roll condition.

          


          

            
Flute angle & temperature

            
Why flute angle + roll temperature matter

            
    
              
  • Smaller flute angle → more shear effect; larger angle → more scraping effect.
    • 
              
  • Example guide: first break flute angle ~8–10%; last breaks ~12–16%.
    • 
              
  • Higher roll speed and harder wheat can generate more heat. Heat causes roll expansion; expansion can be non‑uniform (ends vs center) → uneven grinding.
    • 
              
  • Practical: monitor roll temperature + load trends; ensure proper roll condition/crown and bearing/drive health.
    • 
            

          

        


      


      
      

        3) Mill control (تحكم المطحنة): Break Release + Distribution Table + Granulation
        

          Moved: the Mill control tools are now available in the main “Mill control” card above (easier on mobile).
        

        

          
        

      




        4) Roll wear / refurbishment – impact & what to monitor
        
Bühler – Rolls & Sieves maintenance


        
    
          
  • Worn fluted rolls can shift the mill toward lower‑quality output: low‑ash flours can reduce by up to ~5% (quality loss).
    • 
          
  • Worn rolls can reduce overall yield: extraction can drop by up to ~4%.
    • 
          
  • Specific energy tends to rise with wear; additional energy can also increase moisture loss: ~0.6–1.2% extra moisture loss per additional kWh (guideline).
    • 
          
  • Use trends: amps/kWh·t, granulation, break release, ash curve, and flour in bran/tails to decide refurbishment timing.
    • 
        


        

          Practical idea: if load/energy keeps rising at the same feed + gap, treat it like a maintenance signal (roll surface/corrugation), not only an “operator setting”.
        

      


      

        5) Plansifter sieve condition & tension (PA cloth)
        
Bühler – Rolls & Sieves maintenance


        

          

            
Exchange limits (remaining sieve tension)

            

              
                
Mesh opening (µm)Remaining tension (N/cm)

              
              
                
> 6306

                
600 – 4656

                
450 – 3155

                
300 – 2125

                
200 – 1124

                
< 1124

              
            


            

              Bühler notes that sifting efficiency drops due to sieve loosening: low‑ash flour extraction can be up to ~4% lower.
              With correct tension (e.g., yearly exchange of all flour sieves), total extraction can stay up to ~1% higher. Extra pneumatic relifts can also increase moisture loss (up to ~0.8%).
            

          


          

            
Lookup

            
Tension lookup

            

              
              
            

            

            

              If measured remaining tension is below the value shown, plan a cloth exchange / re‑tensioning service. Also verify brush function and spout cleanliness.
            

          

        

      


      

        6) Reduction rolls surface roughness (Ra) – quick guide
        
Yenar – Reduction rolls reconditioning


        

          

            
Typical roughness ranges

            

              
                
Ra (µm)RemarkShear force

              
              
                
< 1.0Shining rollsLow

                
1.0 – 2.0Very lowLow

                
2.0 – 2.5LowMedium

                
2.5 – 3.0OKMedium

                
3.0 – 3.5GoodHigher

                
> 3.5High (watch starch damage/energy)High

              
            

            
Use as a guide; match to your flour spec, starch damage target, and energy.

          


          

            
Checker

            
Roughness checker

            

              
              
            

            

            
If rolls are too smooth, reduction may under‑grind; if too rough, energy + starch damage can rise.

          

        

      


      

        7) Cleaning & raw wheat bins (capacity + de‑mixing)
        
Swiss School of Milling – Flowsheet Technology


        

          

            
Cleaning sequence (overview)

            
    
              
  • Separate impurities bigger or smaller than wheat + use aspiration to remove light impurities.
    • 
              
  • Separate iron parts (magnets).
    • 
              
  • Separate stones / glass / metal parts (destoner).
    • 
              
  • Separate foreign seeds and foreign cereals.
    • 
              
  • Condition by dampening and tempering.
    • 
              
  • Scour the grain surface (and, if required, impact).
    • 
            


            

              Concept: the cleaning section is often split into a first cleaning section (raw wheat bins → tempering bins) and a second cleaning section (tempering bins → first break).
            


            
Raw wheat bins: reduce segregation

            
    
              
  • Raw wheat bins should hold at least the daily mill capacity (buffer and clearer responsibility between storage & milling).
    • 
              
  • Multiple outlets or double cones help keep a regular lowering of the wheat column and avoid demixing, supporting more constant grist quality.
    • 
            

          


          

            
Calculator

            
Cleaning section hourly capacity


            

              
              
            

            

              
              
            


            


            

              Rule of thumb: the first cleaning section hourly capacity is slightly higher than the mill, so the daily capacity can be processed in about 22–23 hours.
            

          

        


        

          ملخص عربي سريع (قسم التنظيف وصوامع القمح الخام)
          
    
            
  • قسم التنظيف عادة مقسوم إلى: تنظيف أول (من صوامع القمح الخام حتى صوامع التكييف) وتنظيف ثانٍ (من صوامع التكييف حتى الكسرة الأولى).
    • 
            
  • قاعدة عملية: يجب أن تكون قدرة قسم التنظيف أعلى قليلاً من قدرة الطحن حتى تستطيع طحن الكمية اليومية خلال 22–23 ساعة.
    • 
            
  • صوامع القمح الخام بفتحات متعددة أو مخروط مزدوج تساعد على تقليل الانفصال/التدرج (Segregation) والحفاظ على خلطة قمح ثابتة.
    • 
          

        

      


      

        8) Air systems: aspiration, filters, cyclones & airlocks
        
Wheat flour milling – Hibbs & Posner (maintenance concepts)


        

          

            
Why air systems change product & capacity

            
    
              
  • Purifier separation depends on stable exhaust air; restrictions reduce air quantity and can increase recirculation/loads.
    • 
              
  • Dust buildup on filters increases resistance and reduces air quantity (hurts aspiration + pneumatic conveying).
    • 
              
  • Humid air can cause dust to cake on filter cloth, making it hard to clean; inspection and cleaning are important.
    • 
              
  • Cyclones and rotary airlocks are often neglected: airlocks should be airtight. Leakage reduces pressure difference and lowers conveying/aspiration efficiency.
    • 
              
  • Fan wheel buildup can cause imbalance and vibration; inspect regularly.
    • 
            


            

              Troubleshooting tip: when you see “milling” symptoms (overload, choke, poor separation), also check the air system on that specific route.
            

          


          

            
KPI calculator

            
Specific energy (quick)


            

              
              
            

            

              
              
            


            


            

              Use this KPI trend to detect restrictions or wear (compare at similar wheat + settings).
            

          

        


        

          ملخص عربي سريع (الشفط والفلاتر والسيكلون)
          
    
            
  • أي انسداد أو زيادة مقاومة في الفلتر تقلل كمية الهواء → يؤثر على الشفط والنقل الهوائي ونقاوة التنقية (Purifier).
    • 
            
  • تأكد من إحكام Airlock (عدم تسريب) لأن التسريب يقلل فرق الضغط ويضعف الكفاءة.
    • 
            
  • الرطوبة العالية تسبب تكتل غبار على قماش الفلتر وقد يؤدي لانسداد متكرر.
    • 
          

        

      


      

        9) EN ↔ AR glossary (common terms)
        

          
EnglishالعربيةQuick note

          
            
Extraction / Yieldنسبة الاستخراج / المردودكمية الدقيق الناتجة من القمح

            
Ashالرمادمؤشر تلوث الدقيق بالنخالة/الطبقة الاليرونية

            
Bran specksنقاط نخالة / نقاط سوداءجزيئات نخالة صغيرة تظهر في الدقيق

            
Break systemنظام الكسراتفتح الحبة تدريجياً مع الحفاظ على النخالة كبيرة

            
Reduction systemنظام التنعيم / الطحن الناعمطحن السميد/الميدلينغ لإنتاج الدقيق

            
Purifierالمنقّييفصل النخالة عن السميد بالغربلة والهواء

            
Plansifterالبلانسفترغربلة متعددة الطوابق لفصل الأحجام المختلفة

            
Tempering / Conditioningتكييف / ترطيب القمحإضافة ماء + زمن للوصول لاتزان الرطوبة

            
Roll gapفجوة الرولاتالمسافة بين الرولين (تؤثر على التنعيم والطاقة)

            
Roll differentialنسبة السرعةفرق سرعة دوران الرولين (سحب/قص)

          
        

      


      

        All values above are guidelines. Always validate with your wheat, your flowsheet, your product spec, and OEM limits.
      

    


    

      
Logbook (trouble tickets)

      
Save a troubleshooting case linked to your diagram routes. Stored locally on this device (offline).


      


      

        
        
(select a stage)

      


      

        
        
      


      

        
        
      


      

        
        
      


      

        
        
      


      

        
        
      


      

        
        
      


      

        
        
      


      

        
        
      


      

        
        

          
          
        

      


      

        
        
      


      

        
        

          
          
          
          
          
        

      


      

      

    




      
Offline & updates

      
    
        
  • This is a single offline HTML file. Once saved on your iPhone, it works with no internet.
    • 
        
  • For the most “app‑like” feel on iPhone: open in Safari → Share → Add to Home Screen.
    • 
        
  • To update later, replace this HTML file with a newer version.
    • 
      

    

  


  
  

    

      

        

          
Wizard mode

          
Pick symptom -> where -> trace. We'll set the controls and generate the map.

        

        
      

      

        


        

          
1) What is the symptom?

          

            
            
            
            
            
            
            
            
            
            
          

          
Select a symptom to see the recommended trace direction.

        


        

          
2) Where do you notice it first?

          

            
            
            
            
            
            
            
            
          

          
Select where you noticed it first (helps choose what to check next).

        


        

          
3) What direction do you want to trace?

          

            
            
            
          


          

            
            
          

          

        


        

          
4) Apply settings

          

          
This sets Trouble + Trace + Depth (it does not change any mesh sizes or routes). Then it runs Generate.
